_description: D100 table for Character Events. Randomly select one character each turn. All characters eligible including those in Sick Bay. Administrator and grunts are not eligible.
_notes: Characters absent from the campaign entirely (e.g., during R&R event) cannot be targeted. If effects are impossible to implement, ignore the event.

entries.name: Personal Training
entries.description: The character undergoes a training course to hone their mission-relevant skills.

entries.name: Minor Promotion
entries.description: Proficiency is ideally rewarded with improvements in rank and responsibility. The character gains a level of Loyalty and +1 XP. If they were already Loyal, gain +2 XP total.

entries.name: Personal Investigation
entries.description: The character does a bit of information gathering. Randomly choose a Tactical Enemy and receive +1 Enemy Information.

entries.name: Something in the Water, I Suppose
entries.description: The character comes down with a minor illness. If in sick bay, increase recovery time by 1 turn. If not in sick bay, they get a tummy ache but are otherwise fine.

entries.name: R&R
entries.description: The character earns some time off world. The character is gone for 2 campaign turns during which they cannot be the target of events. They recover completely from any injuries. If Disloyal, roll 1D6: 1-2 do not return, 3-5 remain Disloyal, 6 become Committed.

entries.name: Change of Assignment
entries.description: The character is offered a new assignment. If they accept, they immediately earn 5 XP and are transferred to another Five Parsecs game. If they decline, gain 1 level of Loyalty.

entries.name: Disputes with Leadership
entries.description: The character has a heated disagreement with Administration. The character loses a level of Loyalty but cannot drop below Disloyal.

entries.name: Commitment to the Cause
entries.description: The character is satisfied with recent Administration actions and is dedicated to the cause.

entries.name: Dispute
entries.description: A clash of personalities occurs on the team. Randomly pick a second character. The two cannot both be assigned to the mission next campaign turn unless fighting a Pitched Battle.

entries.name: Personal Calibrations
entries.description: Time spent customizing gun sights and ammunition loads pays off. The next time the character is on a mission, select one weapon they carry which receives a +1 hit bonus for that mission.

entries.name: Sickness
entries.description: The character falls ill. They must spend 2 campaign turns recovering.

entries.name: Making Friends
entries.description: The character has spent some quality time with a random character on your roster. They each earn +1 XP and may each roll 1D6: On 5-6, they gain one level of Loyalty.

entries.name: Personal Life Achievement
entries.description: Something significant happens in the character's life (marriage, a new baby, a graduation, etc.)

entries.name: Excellent Health
entries.description: All that fresh air is paying off. If currently recovering from injuries, reduce the time required by 2 turns. If not injured, the effect can be saved.

entries.name: Accident
entries.description: The character injures themselves during work. Roll on the Post-battle Injury table to see how badly they are hurt. If you roll Dead, it instead counts as 5 turns of recovery.

entries.name: Personal Reflection
entries.description: After some hard lessons, the character is determined to learn. The next time they go on a mission, they earn double XP.

entries.name: Personal Conviction
entries.description: If the last mission was a success, gain one level of Loyalty.

entries.name: Losing Faith
entries.description: The character has serious disagreements over the mission and how it is being done. If the last mission was a failure, they lose one level of Loyalty.

entries.name: Personal Tragedy
entries.description: Something happens to throw the character off their game. Next campaign turn they forfeit all XP they would have earned. You may opt to keep them out of a mission to avoid losing out.

entries.name: New Hobby
entries.description: The character has found something new to do in their spare time (sport, amateur band, wood working, miniatures painting). They receive +1 XP and will irritate everyone else.
